leadText: Power-Up Punch (Japanese: グロウパンチ Grow Punch) is a damage-dealing Fighting-type move introduced in Generation VI. It is TM98 in Generation VI and TM018 in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation IX 1.1.1 Pokémon Scarlet and Violet 1.1.2 Pokémon Legends: Z-A Effect [ edit | edit source ] Power-Up Punch inflicts damage and raises the user's Attack by one stage. Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Scarlet and Violet [ edit | edit source ] Power-Up Punch cannot be selected in a battle. Pokémon Legends: Z-A [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Z-A Category Physical Accuracy 100% Standard Plus Move Power 40 48 Cooldown 10s Move wind-up time 15 frames Effect duration 4.5s Additional effect — — — None

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA Striking opponents over and over makes the user's fists harder. Hitting a target raises the Attack stat. S V This move can't be used. It's recommended that this move is forgotten. Once forgotten, this move can't be remembered. ZA The user attacks targets with a punch that will make its own fist tougher. This boosts the user's Attack stat for a short time.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description S MD It damages an enemy. It could also boost your Attack. The stat returns to normal when you go to the next floor or step on a Wonder Tile.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Wooper , Pupitar , and Fennekin are all able learn Power-Up Punch despite not having arms. In the case of Pupitar, this can be explained by the fact that it evolves from a Pokémon that already has arms .
